# Let's implement normalization & similarity and search the hadith DB and quran DB
import re, unicodedata
def normalize_arabic(s):
    # Strip tashkeel (diacritics) and tatweel, punctuation; unify hamza forms and alef variations
    # Lowercase doesn't really apply, but we'll keep
    # Remove diacritics (tashkeel)
    tashkeel_pattern = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06DC\u06DF-\u06E8\u06EA-\u06ED]')
    s2 = tashkeel_pattern.sub('', s)
    # Remove tatweel
    s2 = s2.replace('\u0640','')
    # normalize alef variants to bare alif 'ا'
    s2 = re.sub('[إأآا]', 'ا', s2)
    # normalize hamza forms to 'ء' or maybe to 'ا'?? Instruction: unify hamza; let's replace hamza on waw/yah etc.
    s2 = re.sub('[ؤئ]', 'ء', s2)
    # remove punctuation and symbols (keep Arabic letters and numbers)
    s2 = re.sub(r'[^\w\u0600-\u06FF\s]', ' ', s2)  # allow Arabic letters and digits and underscore
    # underscores may remain from \w, remove underscores
    s2 = s2.replace('_',' ')
    # collapse whitespace
    s2 = re.sub(r'\s+',' ', s2).strip()
    # lowercase - not applicable for Arabic but fine
    return s2

def token_f1(a,b):
    # a,b are normalized strings; tokens by space
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    if len(ta)==0 or len(tb)==0:
        return 0.0
    sa = set(ta)
    sb = set(tb)
    overlap = sa & sb  # token overlap set
    # But use multiset? Could use counts. For token F1, better to use multiset intersection counts.
    from collections import Counter
    ca = Counter(ta)
    cb = Counter(tb)
    inter = 0
    for t in (set(ta) & set(tb)):
        inter += min(ca[t], cb[t])
    precision = inter / len(ta)
    recall = inter / len(tb)
    if precision+recall==0:
        return 0.0
    return 2*precision*recall/(precision+recall), precision, recall

# LCS on character normalized strings
def lcs_ratio(a,b):
    # dynamic programming LCS length / max(len(a),len(b))
    # For speed, if lengths > 1000, use approximate.
    la = len(a); lb = len(b)
    if la==0 or lb==0:
        return 0.0
    # compute lcs length
    # Using dynamic programming O(la*lb) which might be big; but our strings are short
    dp = [[0]*(lb+1) for _ in range(la+1)]
    for i,ch in enumerate(a,1):
        for j,chb in enumerate(b,1):
            if ch==chb:
                dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]+1
            else:
                dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] if dp[i-1][j] >= dp[i][j-1] else dp[i][j-1]
    lcs_len = dp[la][lb]
    return lcs_len/max(la,lb), lcs_len

# Prepare query strings: full substring extracted and provided snippet
full_sub = substring  # from response_context substring earlier
snippet = "إنَّ العِل... ريبٌ، وال"  # provided_snippet
# But the provided snippet might have ellipsis etc. Normalize both: remove ellipsis '...'
snippet_clean = snippet.replace('...',' ')
print("Full_sub:", repr(full_sub))

norm_snip = normalize_arabic(snippet_clean)
print("Normalized full:", norm_full)
print("Normalized snippet:", norm_snip)

# Improved normalization
import unicodedata
import regex as re  # better unicode support, but regex module might not be available. Let's try standard re and unicodedata.

def normalize_arabic2(s):
    # Remove diacritics via unicode category Mn (Mark, nonspacing)
    s2 = ''.join(ch for ch in unicodedata.normalize('NFC', s) if unicodedata.category(ch) != 'Mn')
    # Remove tatweel
    s2 = s2.replace('\u0640','')
    # Normalize alef variants to bare alif 'ا'
    s2 = re.sub('[إأآا]', 'ا', s2)
    # Normalize hamza variants to 'ء'
    s2 = re.sub('[ؤئ]', 'ء', s2)
    # Remove punctuation characters (unicode category starts with P) and symbols (S)
    s3 = ''.join(ch if (unicodedata.category(ch)[0] not in ('P','S')) else ' ' for ch in s2)
    # Remove extra spaces
    s3 = re.sub(r'\s+',' ', s3).strip()
    return s3

# test normalization
print(normalize_arabic2(full_sub))
print(normalize_arabic2(snippet_clean))

# Build list of candidates and compute similarity to norm_full
norm_full = normalize_arabic2(full_sub)
norm_snip = normalize_arabic2(snippet_clean)

best = []
for entry in data:
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    if not cand:
        continue
    norm_cand = normalize_arabic2(cand)
    f1,prec,rec = token_f1(norm_cand, norm_full)
    lcs_r, lcs_len = lcs_ratio(norm_cand, norm_full)
    sim = max(f1, lcs_r)
    if sim > 0:
        best.append((sim, f1, lcs_r, prec, rec, lcs_len, entry, cand, norm_cand))
# sort by sim desc
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
# print top 20
for i in range(20):
    s = best_sorted[i]
    print(i+1, "sim", s[0], "f1", s[1], "lcs", s[2], "prec", s[3], "rec", s[4], "lcs_len", s[5])
    # print candidate sample (first 150 chars)
    print("cand:", s[7][:200].replace("\n"," "), "...")
    print("norm_cand:", s[8][:200])
    print("BookID/title:", s[6].get('BookID'), s[6].get('title'))
    print("----")

def token_f1(a,b):
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    if len(ta)==0 or len(tb)==0:
        return 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
    from collections import Counter
    ca = Counter(ta)
    cb = Counter(tb)
    inter = 0
    for t in (set(ta) & set(tb)):
        inter += min(ca[t], cb[t])
    precision = inter / len(ta)
    recall = inter / len(tb)
    if precision+recall==0:
        return 0.0, precision, recall
    return 2*precision*recall/(precision+recall), precision, recall

# re-run scan
best = []
for entry in data:
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    if not cand:
        continue
    norm_cand = normalize_arabic2(cand)
    f1,prec,rec = token_f1(norm_cand, norm_full)
    lcs_r, lcs_len = lcs_ratio(norm_cand, norm_full)
    sim = max(f1, lcs_r)
    if sim > 0:
        best.append((sim, f1, lcs_r, prec, rec, lcs_len, entry, cand, norm_cand))
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
for i in range(20):
    s = best_sorted[i]
    print(i+1, "sim", s[0], "f1", s[1], "lcs", s[2], "prec", s[3], "rec", s[4], "lcs_len", s[5])
    print("cand:", s[7][:200].replace("\n"," "), "...")
    print("norm_cand:", s[8][:200])
    print("BookID/title:", s[6].get('BookID'), s[6].get('title'))
    print("----")

query = "إنَّ العِلمَ يَقينٌ، واليقينُ لا يزولُ، وإنَّ الشكَّ ريبٌ، والريبُ لا يزولُ"

norm_q

for v in qdata:
